questions.question: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre took place on:
questions.explanation: On 13 April 1919 (Baisakhi Day), British Brigadier General Reginald Dyer ordered troops to fire on a peaceful gathering at Jallianwala Bagh, Amritsar. Hundreds were killed.

questions.question: The concept of 'Ahimsa' (non-violence) as a political tool was first applied by Gandhi in:
questions.explanation: Gandhi first applied non-violent resistance (Satyagraha) in South Africa (1906–1914) while fighting for the rights of Indian immigrants. This experience shaped his philosophy for India's independence struggle.

questions.question: The Aryans are believed to have migrated into India through the:
options: Khyber and Bolan passes from Central Asia
Arabian Sea from the Middle East
questions.explanation: The Indo-Aryans are believed to have entered India through the northwestern mountain passes (Khyber, Bolan) from Central Asia around 1500 BCE, though the exact nature of migration is debated.

questions.question: The oldest of the four Vedas is:
questions.explanation: The Rigveda (c. 1500–1200 BCE) is the oldest of the four Vedas, containing 1028 hymns (suktas) arranged in 10 mandalas. It is one of the oldest religious texts in the world.

questions.question: The 'Gayatri Mantra' is from:
questions.explanation: The Gayatri Mantra (Savitri Mantra) is from the Rigveda (3rd mandala, hymn 62, verse 10). It invokes the solar deity Savitr. It is considered the most sacred mantra in Hinduism.

questions.question: The Later Vedic period saw the shift of Aryan culture from Punjab to:
questions.explanation: In the Later Vedic period (c. 1000–600 BCE), the centre of Aryan culture shifted from the Punjab to the Gangetic plain (between Ganga and Yamuna), known as the Doab or 'Madhyadesha'.

questions.question: The Varna system in early Vedic society was based on:
options: Occupation (karma) — it was not hereditary initially
questions.explanation: In the Rigvedic period, the varna system was based on occupation, not birth. The four varnas were Brahmin (priest), Kshatriya (warrior), Vaishya (trader), Shudra (servant). Hereditary rigidity came in the Later Vedic period.

questions.question: The Purusha Sukta, which describes the creation of the four varnas, is in:
questions.explanation: The Purusha Sukta (Rigveda, 10th Mandala, Hymn 90) describes the cosmic being Purusha's sacrifice: Brahmins from his mouth, Kshatriyas from arms, Vaishyas from thighs, Shudras from feet.

questions.question: The Upanishads deal with:
options: Philosophical and metaphysical ideas (Brahman, Atman, Maya, Karma)
questions.explanation: The Upanishads (c. 800–200 BCE) are philosophical texts exploring the nature of ultimate reality (Brahman), individual soul (Atman), and their identity. They form the basis of Vedanta philosophy.

questions.question: The epic 'Mahabharata' was originally composed by:
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata was composed by Vyasa (also called Krishna Dvaipayana or Veda Vyasa). The original was called 'Jaya' (8,800 verses), expanded to 'Bharata' (24,000), then 'Mahabharata' (100,000 verses).

questions.question: The five vows (Pancha Mahavrata) of Jainism include all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: The five Mahavrata of Jainism: Ahimsa, Satya, Asteya (non-theft), Brahmacharya (celibacy), and Aparigraha (non-possession). 'Ahara' (food) is not one of them. Brahmacharya was added by Mahavira to Parshvanatha's four vows.

questions.question: Gayatri Mantra is found in:
questions.explanation: Gayatri Mantra (dedicated to solar deity Savitri) is found in the Rigveda (3.62.10). It is the most sacred mantra in Hinduism.

questions.question: Sabha and Samiti in Vedic period were:
questions.explanation: Sabha and Samiti were tribal assemblies in the Vedic period. Sabha was a smaller, exclusive council of elders; Samiti was a larger, more democratic gathering.

questions.question: Iron was first used in India during:
questions.explanation: Iron came into widespread use in India during the Later Vedic period (1000-600 BCE), transforming agriculture, warfare, and craftsmanship.

questions.question: Purushmedha refers to:
questions.explanation: Purushmedha was a ritual human sacrifice described in Vedic texts, though scholars debate whether it was actually practiced or purely symbolic.

questions.question: Triratna of Jainism refers to:
options: Right Faith, Right Knowledge, Right Conduct
questions.explanation: Jain Triratna (Three Jewels): Right Faith (Samyak Darshan), Right Knowledge (Samyak Jnana), Right Conduct (Samyak Charitra) — the path to liberation.

questions.question: Theravada Buddhism is also called:
questions.explanation: Theravada (Way of the Elders) Buddhism is also called Hinayana (Lesser Vehicle) by Mahayana Buddhists. It emphasizes personal salvation and the original Pali Canon.

questions.question: Aryabhata wrote the famous work:
questions.explanation: Aryabhata (476-550 CE) wrote Aryabhatiya — covering arithmetic, algebra, plane geometry, spherical trigonometry. He correctly calculated value of π and explained solar/lunar eclipses.

questions.question: Bal Gangadhar Tilak gave the slogan:
questions.explanation: 'Swarajya is my birthright and I shall have it' was the famous slogan of Bal Gangadhar Tilak, the fiery nationalist who also started the Ganapati and Shivaji festivals for national awakening.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Mudrarakshasa'?
questions.explanation: Mudrarakshasa by Vishakhadatta is a Sanskrit play depicting the political intrigue after Chandragupta Maurya's rise to power, with Chanakya as the main character.

questions.question: The Nagara style of temple architecture is mainly found in:
questions.explanation: Nagara style (North Indian/Indo-Aryan) has a curvilinear tower (shikhara) over the sanctum. Dravidian style (South Indian) has a pyramidal tower (vimana). Vesara is a mix of both.

questions.question: The Vellore Mutiny of 1806 was the first:
options: Sepoy revolt against British in India
questions.explanation: Vellore Mutiny (10 July 1806) was the first major sepoy mutiny against the British, 51 years before the 1857 revolt, triggered by new dress regulations seen as an attack on religion.

questions.question: 'Janpad' in ancient India referred to:
questions.explanation: Janapada (jana=tribe/people, pada=foot/territory) refers to the territorial states that developed in the Later Vedic period. 16 great Mahajanapadas existed around 600 BCE.

questions.question: The 'Grand Trunk Road' was built by:
questions.explanation: Grand Trunk Road from Chittagong to Kabul was built/renovated by Sher Shah Suri (16th century). Originally an ancient road, Sher Shah restored and extended it, planting trees and building sarais.

questions.question: The Dravidian Movement in Tamil Nadu was influenced significantly by which organization?
questions.explanation: The Justice Party (South Indian Liberal Federation, 1916) was the forerunner of the Dravidian movement. It championed non-Brahmin rights and eventually evolved into the Dravidian parties.

questions.question: The Nataraja (dancing Shiva) bronze is a Chola masterpiece. What does the cosmic dance (Ananda Tandava) symbolise?
options: The five cosmic activities: creation, preservation, destruction, concealment, grace
questions.explanation: Nataraja's cosmic dance symbolises Panchakriya: Srishti (creation — right hand's raised drum), Sthiti (preservation — right hand's abhaya pose), Samhara (destruction — left hand with fire), Tirobhava (concealment — foot on dwarf Apasmara/ignorance), Anugraha (liberation — raised left foot).

questions.question: The invasion of Timur (Tamerlane) devastated Delhi in:
questions.explanation: Timur (Tamerlane) of Samarkand invaded India and sacked Delhi in 1398 CE, massacring 100,000+ prisoners and looting enormous wealth. This destroyed the Tughlaq dynasty and led to the Sayyid and Lodi dynasties.

questions.question: Raja Ram Mohan Roy founded the Brahmo Samaj in:
questions.explanation: Brahmo Samaj was founded by Raja Ram Mohan Roy on 20 August 1828 in Calcutta. It promoted monotheism, rationalism, and social reforms like abolition of sati.

questions.question: The Arya Samaj was founded by:
questions.explanation: Swami Dayananda Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj in 1875 at Bombay. Its motto: 'Go Back to the Vedas'. It opposed idol worship, casteism, and promoted education.

questions.question: Jyotirao Phule founded:
questions.explanation: Jyotirao Phule founded Satyashodhak Samaj (Truth-Seekers' Society) in 1873 in Pune to fight caste discrimination and promote social equality for lower castes and women.

questions.question: The Gulf War (1991) was triggered by:
questions.explanation: The First Gulf War (1990-91) was triggered by Iraq's invasion and annexation of Kuwait (2 August 1990). A US-led coalition liberated Kuwait in Operation Desert Storm.

questions.question: The Permanent Settlement (1793) of land revenue was introduced by:
questions.explanation: Lord Cornwallis introduced the Permanent Settlement in Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa (1793). It fixed land revenue permanently with zamindars who became hereditary owners. Peasants (ryots) had no land rights — caused widespread impoverishment.

questions.question: The 'Vaikom Satyagraha' (1924-25) was about:
options: Right of untouchables to walk on public roads near Vaikom temple (Travancore/Kerala)
questions.explanation: The Vaikom Satyagraha (1924–25) demanded the right of lower-caste people (Ezhavas, Pulayas) to walk on public roads near the Vaikom Mahadeva Temple in Travancore (Kerala). Periyar was a key participant. The roads were eventually opened.

questions.question: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule founded which organisation for lower-caste upliftment?
questions.explanation: Jyotirao Phule (1827–1890) founded the Satya Shodhak Samaj (Truth-Seeking Society) in 1873 in Pune. He fought against Brahminical dominance, opened the first school for girls in India (with his wife Savitribai), and wrote Gulamgiri (Slavery).

questions.question: The Arya Samaj was founded by Dayananda Saraswati in:
questions.explanation: Dayananda Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj in Bombay (1875). It advocated return to Vedic religion, rejected idol worship, caste by birth, and child marriage. It also started the Shuddhi (reconversion) movement.

questions.question: The book 'Satyarth Prakash' was written by:
questions.explanation: Satyarth Prakash ('Light of Truth', 1875) was written by Dayananda Saraswati. It outlined the principles of the Arya Samaj — monotheism, Vedic authority, rejection of idol worship, child marriage, and untouchability.

questions.question: The INA (Indian National Army) was originally organised by:
options: Mohan Singh (1942) and later reorganised by Subhas Chandra Bose (1943)
questions.explanation: INA was first organised by Captain Mohan Singh from Indian POWs captured by Japan in Malaya (1942). Subhas Chandra Bose took over leadership in 1943, reorganised it, declared Provisional Government of Free India, and launched the Imphal-Kohima offensive.

questions.question: Subramania Bharati (Mahakavi Bharati) was a famous Tamil:
options: Freedom fighter and poet who wrote revolutionary Tamil poems and songs
questions.explanation: Subramania Bharati (1882–1921) was a Tamil poet and freedom fighter. His songs ('Vande Mataram' translation, 'Kuyil Pattu', 'Kannan Pattu') inspired the freedom movement. He advocated women's liberation, Dalit rights, and national unity.

questions.question: Bal Gangadhar Tilak's famous saying was:
options: 'Give me blood and I will give you freedom'
'Swaraj is my birthright and I shall have it'
questions.explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak's famous declaration 'Swaraj is my birthright and I shall have it' inspired the extremist nationalism. He used festivals (Ganesh Chaturthi, Shivaji Jayanti) to spread the freedom movement.

questions.question: The Vellore Mutiny (1806) was the first major armed uprising against the British in South India. It was triggered by:
options: New military regulations (turbans, earrings, tilaks banned) — seen as attempts to Christianise soldiers
questions.explanation: The Vellore Mutiny (10 July 1806) was triggered by new uniform regulations that banned turbans, facial marks, and earrings for sepoys (soldiers), which they saw as forced Christianisation. The mutiny was suppressed; over 100 sepoys were executed.

questions.question: The Vaikam Satyagraha (1924-25) was led by:
questions.explanation: Vaikam Satyagraha was launched in 1924 to win the right of lower castes to walk on roads near the Vaikam temple in Kerala. T.K. Madhavan initiated it; Periyar played key role.

questions.question: The Tuticorin (Thoothukudi) Mass Agitation of 1908 was led by:
questions.explanation: V.O. Chidambaram Pillai and Subramania Siva led the Tuticorin mass agitation in 1908, connected to the Swadeshi movement. VOC was sentenced to 6 years rigorous imprisonment.

questions.question: The First Five-Year Plan of India focused on:
questions.explanation: India's First Five-Year Plan (1951-56) was based on the Harrod-Domar model and focused on agriculture, irrigation, power, and rehabilitation after partition.

questions.question: World War I was triggered by:
options: Assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria-Hungary at Sarajevo (28 June 1914)
questions.explanation: WWI (1914–1918) was triggered by the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand (heir to Austria-Hungary) by Gavrilo Princip (Serbian nationalist) in Sarajevo, Bosnia. This set off the alliance system and led to a global conflict.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is associated with the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization is associated with the Indus river. It was a major river of the civilization.

questions.question: What was the primary occupation of people during the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: Pastoralism was the primary occupation of people during the Vedic age. They were mainly cattle rearers.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mauryan dynasty?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya was the founder of the Mauryan dynasty. He founded the dynasty in 322 BCE.

questions.question: Which of the following South Indian kingdoms built the Brihadeeswara temple?
questions.explanation: The Cholas built the Brihadeeswara temple. It was built during the reign of Rajaraja Chola.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Vijayanagara empire?
questions.explanation: Harihara was the founder of the Vijayanagara empire. He founded the empire in 1336 CE.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major consequence of the British conquest of India?
questions.explanation: Exploitation was a major consequence of the British conquest of India. The British exploited India's resources.

questions.question: Who was a prominent leader of the socio-reform movement in India?
questions.explanation: Raja Ram Mohan Roy was a prominent leader of the socio-reform movement in India. He fought against social evils.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major event in the freedom struggle of India?
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was a major event in the freedom struggle of India. It was led by Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Maratha empire?
questions.explanation: Shivaji was the founder of the Maratha empire. He founded the empire in 1674 CE.

questions.question: Which of the following dynasties ruled Tamil Nadu during the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: The Aryans ruled Tamil Nadu during the Vedic age. They migrated to Tamil Nadu from the north.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mauryan empire?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Mauryan empire. He was the first emperor.

questions.question: Which dynasty built the famous Shore Temple in Mahabalipuram?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas built the Shore Temple. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: What was the name of the famous king of the Vijayanagara empire who built the Virupaksha Temple?
questions.explanation: Krishnadeva Raya built the Virupaksha Temple. He was a prominent king.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Maratha Confederacy?
questions.explanation: Shivaji was the leader of the Maratha Confederacy. He founded the Maratha empire.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Brahmo Samaj socio-reform movement?
questions.explanation: Raja Ram Mohan Roy founded the Brahmo Samaj. He was a prominent reformer.

questions.question: Which of the following was a key event in the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: The Salt March was a key event in the Indian freedom struggle. It was led by Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.question: Who was the first Governor-General of India under the British East India Company?
questions.explanation: Warren Hastings was the first Governor-General of India. He played a key role in British India.

questions.question: Which of the following Vedas is the oldest?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda is the oldest of the four Vedas. It contains hymns and prayers.

questions.question: Which South Indian kingdom built the temple of Brihadeeswarar?
questions.explanation: The Cholas built the temple of Brihadeeswarar. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major feature of the Vedic society?
questions.explanation: The caste system was a major feature of the Vedic society. It was a social hierarchy.

questions.question: Which civilization is known for its urban planning and architecture during the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization is known for its urban planning and architecture. It flourished around 2500 BCE.

questions.question: What is the name of the river mentioned in the Rigveda as the eastern boundary of the Vedic civilization?
questions.explanation: The Ganges river is mentioned in the Rigveda as the eastern boundary. It is a sacred river in Hinduism.

questions.question: Who is the author of the Vedas according to Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: The Vedas are considered to be of divine origin. They are attributed to God.

questions.question: What was the primary occupation of the people during the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: Pastoralism was the primary occupation during the Vedic age. People were nomadic herders.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Vedic society?
questions.explanation: The Vedic society was patriarchal. Men held positions of power.

questions.question: What is the name of the most ancient Veda?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda is the most ancient Veda. It contains hymns and prayers.

questions.question: Who were the ruling class during the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: The Kshatriyas were the ruling class during the Vedic age. They were warriors and rulers.

questions.question: What was the name of the Vedic god of fire?
questions.explanation: Agni is the Vedic god of fire. He is often invoked in rituals.

questions.question: Which of the following was a feature of the Vedic economy?
questions.explanation: The Vedic economy was based on a barter system. Goods were exchanged for other goods.

questions.question: What is the name of the Vedic ritual of sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Yajna is the Vedic ritual of sacrifice. It involves offerings to the gods.

questions.question: Who is considered the founder of the Mauryan Empire?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya is considered the founder of the Mauryan Empire. He ruled from 322-298 BCE.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Maratha Confederacy during the Third Anglo-Maratha War?
questions.explanation: Maharao Holkar was the leader of the Maratha Confederacy during the Third Anglo-Maratha War. The war took place from 1816-1819.

questions.question: Which of the following social reform movements aimed at uplifting the status of women in India?
questions.explanation: All the mentioned social reform movements aimed at uplifting the status of women in India. They worked towards women's education and rights.

questions.question: Who is known as the Father of the Nation in India?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi is known as the Father of the Nation in India. He played a key role in the Indian freedom struggle.

questions.question: Which of the following was a key factor that led to the British conquest of India?
questions.explanation: All the mentioned factors led to the British conquest of India. They exploited India's resources and took advantage of its political instability.

questions.question: What was the name of the movement launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1942 demanding complete independence from British rule?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1942. It demanded complete independence from British rule.

questions.question: Who is the author of the Rigveda?
questions.explanation: The author of the Rigveda is unknown. It is a collection of Vedic Sanskrit hymns.

questions.question: What was the name of the largest city of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: Mohenjo-Daro was the largest city of the Indus Valley Civilization. It was a major urban center.

questions.question: Which of the following civilizations is associated with the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: The Vedic age is associated with the Vedic civilization, which is named after the Vedas, the sacred texts of Hinduism.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is mentioned in the Rigveda?
questions.explanation: The Saraswati river is mentioned in the Rigveda, one of the most sacred texts of Hinduism.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the Vedic society?
questions.explanation: The Vedic society was primarily patriarchal, with men holding significant social and religious authority.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is associated with the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: The Vedic age is associated with the Ganges river, which was considered sacred. The Ganges river played a significant role in the Vedic civilization.

questions.question: Which of the following Indus Valley cities is known for its planned architecture?
questions.explanation: Mohenjo-Daro is known for its planned architecture with advanced drainage system.

questions.question: The Vedic age can be dated to which of the following periods?
questions.explanation: The Vedic age is generally dated to 1500 BCE - 500 BCE.

questions.question: The Upanishads are a part of which Veda?
questions.explanation: The Upanishads are a part of the Rigveda and other Vedas.

questions.question: The concept of 'Dharma' originated in which period?
questions.explanation: The concept of Dharma originated in the Vedic period.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major occupation of the people during the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: Pastoralism was a major occupation of the people during the Vedic age.

questions.question: The Vedic people worshipped which of the following gods?
questions.explanation: Indra was one of the primary gods worshipped by the Vedic people.

questions.question: The term 'Veda' means which of the following?
questions.explanation: The term Veda means knowledge or sacred text.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is believed to be the center of the Vedic civilization?
questions.explanation: The Saraswati river is considered the center of the Vedic civilization, as mentioned in the Rigveda.

questions.question: Which of the following Vedic gods was considered the god of thunder?
questions.explanation: Indra was considered the god of thunder and the king of the gods in the Vedic period.

questions.question: The Vedic period is divided into two phases: the Early Vedic period and the?
questions.explanation: The Vedic period is divided into two phases: the Early Vedic period and the Later Vedic period.

questions.question: Which of the following was a notable feature of the Vedic society?
questions.explanation: The Vedic society was primarily a patriarchal system, with men holding significant authority.

questions.question: The Vedas are considered the?
questions.explanation: The Vedas are considered the primary scriptures of Hinduism.

questions.question: Which of the following Vedic texts is considered the oldest?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda is considered the oldest of the Vedic texts.

questions.question: The Vedic age is characterized by the use of?
questions.explanation: The Vedic age is characterized by the use of bronze tools and the development of a bronze technology.

questions.question: Which of the following was a significant aspect of the Vedic economy?
questions.explanation: Agriculture and pastoralism were significant aspects of the Vedic economy.

questions.question: The Vedic period saw the emergence of?
questions.explanation: The Vedic period saw the emergence of small tribal settlements, with the tribe being the primary unit of society.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major characteristic of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization is known for its sophisticated urban planning, with well-planned cities and advanced drainage systems.

questions.question: Who is considered the founder of Buddhism?
questions.explanation: Gautama Buddha is the founder of Buddhism, born in Lumbini, Nepal.

questions.question: The Vedas are divided into how many parts?
questions.explanation: The Vedas are divided into 4 parts: Rigveda, Yajurveda, Samaveda, and Atharvaveda.

questions.question: The Mauryan emperor who converted to Buddhism was?
questions.explanation: Ashoka, the grandson of Chandragupta Maurya, converted to Buddhism after the Kalinga War.

questions.question: The capital of the Pallava kingdom was?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram was the capital of the Pallava kingdom, known for its ancient temples and sculptures.

questions.question: The Vijayanagara kingdom was founded by?
questions.explanation: Harihara and Bukka, two brothers, founded the Vijayanagara kingdom in 1336.

questions.question: The Chola kingdom was known for its?
questions.explanation: The Chola kingdom was known for its extensive maritime trade with Southeast Asia and China.

questions.question: The Maratha kingdom was founded by?
questions.explanation: Shivaji, a renowned warrior, founded the Maratha kingdom in 1674.

questions.question: The British East India Company established its first factory in India at?
questions.explanation: The British East India Company established its first factory in India at Surat in 1612.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 by A.O. Hume and others.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is mentioned in the Rigveda as the easternmost boundary of the Vedic kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Sutlej river is mentioned in the Rigveda as the easternmost boundary of the Vedic kingdom. It is one of the tributaries of the Indus river.

questions.question: The Vedic age is divided into how many periods?
questions.explanation: The Vedic age is divided into two periods: the Early Vedic period and the Late Vedic period.

questions.question: Who is the main deity in the Rigveda?
questions.explanation: Indra is the main deity in the Rigveda, worshipped as the king of the gods.

questions.question: What was the name of the social class that consisted of warriors and nobles in the Vedic society?
questions.explanation: The Kshatriyas were the social class that consisted of warriors and nobles in the Vedic society.

questions.question: The Vedas were composed in which language?
questions.explanation: The Vedas were composed in Sanskrit, an ancient Indian language.

questions.question: Which of the following Vedic gods was associated with fire?
questions.explanation: Agni is the Vedic god associated with fire, worshipped as the messenger of the gods.

questions.question: What was the name of the Vedic ritual that involved the sacrifice of horses?
questions.explanation: The Ashvamedha was a Vedic ritual that involved the sacrifice of horses to establish the supremacy of a king.

questions.question: Which of the following was a characteristic of the Vedic economy?
questions.explanation: Pastoralism was a characteristic of the Vedic economy, with people relying on cattle rearing and agriculture.

questions.question: Which of the following is a notable feature of the Vedic society?
questions.explanation: The Vedic society was a patriarchal society.

questions.question: The Gupta period is known as the?
questions.explanation: The Gupta period is known as the Golden Age.

questions.question: Which of the following South Indian kingdoms built the shore temple at Mahabalipuram?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas built the shore temple at Mahabalipuram.

questions.question: The Vijayanagara empire was founded by?
questions.explanation: The Vijayanagara empire was founded by Harihara and Bukka.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is believed to be the sacred river of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization is believed to have flourished around the river Sindhu. The river Sindhu is also known as the Indus river.

questions.question: Who is considered the founder of the Mauryan dynasty?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya is considered the founder of the Mauryan dynasty. He founded the dynasty in 322 BCE.

questions.question: Which South Indian kingdom was known for its powerful navy?
questions.explanation: The Chola kingdom was known for its powerful navy. They had a strong maritime trade.

questions.question: What was the capital of the Vijayanagara empire?
questions.explanation: Hampi was the capital of the Vijayanagara empire. It was a powerful empire in South India.

questions.question: Which of the following was a result of the British conquest of India?
questions.explanation: The British conquest of India led to the exploitation of its resources. The British exploited India's resources for their own benefit.

questions.question: Who is considered the father of the Indian socio-reform movement?
questions.explanation: Raja Ram Mohan Roy is considered the father of the Indian socio-reform movement. He fought against social evils like sati.

questions.question: What was the main goal of the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: The main goal of the Indian freedom struggle was to achieve independence from British rule. Indians wanted to be free from British rule.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is believed to be the origin of the Vedic civilization?
questions.explanation: The Vedic civilization is believed to have originated near the Sindhu river. Sindhu is another name for the Indus river.

questions.question: Which of the following Vedas is dedicated to the rituals and sacrifices of the Vedic people?
questions.explanation: The Yajurveda is dedicated to the rituals and sacrifices of the Vedic people, containing formulas and rituals for sacrifices.

questions.question: Who was the famous Vedic sage and author of the Rigveda?
questions.explanation: Vasistha was a famous Vedic sage and author of several suktas in the Rigveda.

questions.question: What was the name of the social class system that emerged during the Vedic age?
questions.explanation: The Varna system was the social class system that emerged during the Vedic age, dividing society into four varnas.

questions.question: Which of the following kingdoms was a major power during the post-Vedic period?
questions.explanation: Magadha was a major power during the post-Vedic period, eventually giving rise to the Mauryan empire.

questions.question: Which of the following was a key feature of the Vedic economy?
questions.explanation: The barter system was a key feature of the Vedic economy, with goods and services being exchanged for other goods and services.

questions.explanation: The Chola dynasty built impressive temples. These temples are renowned for their architecture.

questions.question: The Vijayanagara Empire, which flourished from 1336 to 1646 CE, was founded by which of the following brothers?
questions.explanation: Harihara and Bukka founded the Vijayanagara Empire. They were brothers.

questions.question: The Maratha Empire, which rose to power in the 17th century CE, was founded by which of the following leaders?
questions.explanation: Shivaji founded the Maratha Empire. He is considered a great leader.

questions.question: The British conquest of India was marked by the establishment of which of the following companies in 1600 CE?
questions.explanation: The East India Company was established in 1600. It played a significant role in the British conquest.

questions.question: The socio-reform movements in India during the 19th and 20th centuries CE were led by which of the following figures?
questions.explanation: Rammohan Roy led socio-reform movements. He advocated for social change.

questions.question: The Indian freedom struggle was marked by the participation of which of the following prominent leaders?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was a prominent leader in the Indian freedom struggle. He advocated for non-violence.
